git.diffstat:
src/agent_loom/pack/core.py  |  9 +++++-
 tests/test_pack_cli_ux.py    | 17 +++++++++++
 tests/test_pack_lifecycle.py | 68 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++-
 3 files changed, 92 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)
git.patch:
diff --git a/src/agent_loom/pack/core.py b/src/agent_loom/pack/core.py
index 0943b87..c0c35be 100644
--- a/src/agent_loom/pack/core.py
+++ b/src/agent_loom/pack/core.py
@@ -193,13 +193,15 @@ def install_pack(
         sha_src = sha256_file(src)
         if dst.exists() and not force:
             # If the file already matches the pack, adopt it without rewriting.
-            # Otherwise, treat it as drift and do not take ownership.
+            # Otherwise, treat it as drift but still record the expected pack hash
+            # so status/doctor can report persistent drift and users can inspect diffs.
             if sha256_file(dst) == sha_src:
                 skipped.append(rel)
                 installed_files.append(LockFileEntry(path=rel, sha256=sha_src))
             else:
                 skipped.append(rel)
                 drifted.append(rel)
+                installed_files.append(LockFileEntry(path=rel, sha256=sha_src))
             continue
 
         ensure_parent_dir(dst, dry_run=dry_run)
@@ -219,6 +221,9 @@ def install_pack(
     )
     save_lock(repo_root, lock, dry_run=dry_run)
 
+    if drifted and not force:
+        warnings.append("some files drifted; rerun with --force to overwrite")
+
     return PackApplyResult(
         ok=True,
         action="install",
@@ -328,6 +333,8 @@ def update_pack(
             drifted.append(rel)
             if not force:
                 skipped.append(rel)
+                # Record expected pack hash so drift is persistent + diffable.
+                installed_files.append(LockFileEntry(path=rel, sha256=sha_src))
                 continue
 
             ensure_parent_dir(dst, dry_run=dry_run)
diff --git a/tests/test_pack_cli_ux.py b/tests/test_pack_cli_ux.py
index 6285652..7c64b15 100644
--- a/tests/test_pack_cli_ux.py
+++ b/tests/test_pack_cli_ux.py
@@ -1,6 +1,7 @@
 import contextlib
 import importlib
 import io
+import json
 import os
 import tempfile
 import unittest
@@ -57,6 +58,22 @@ class TestPackCliUx(unittest.TestCase):
         self.assertIn("--- pack:sample/.opencode/commands/pack-sample.md", out)
         self.assertIn("+drift", out)
 
+    def test_install_conflict_is_visible_in_status(self) -> None:
+        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
+            repo = Path(td)
+
+            p = repo / ".opencode" / "commands" / "pack-sample.md"
+            p.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+            p.write_text("preexisting drift\n", encoding="utf-8")
+
+            install_pack(repo_root=repo, pack_id="sample", dry_run=False)
+
+            rc, out, err = _run_text(["status", "--json"], cwd=repo)
+        self.assertEqual(rc, 0)
+        self.assertEqual(err, "")
+        payload = json.loads(out.strip())
+        self.assertEqual(int(payload.get("drifted") or 0), 1)
+
 
 if __name__ == "__main__":
     unittest.main()
diff --git a/tests/test_pack_lifecycle.py b/tests/test_pack_lifecycle.py
index a040d1f..6480d93 100644
--- a/tests/test_pack_lifecycle.py
+++ b/tests/test_pack_lifecycle.py
@@ -3,9 +3,16 @@ from __future__ import annotations
 import json
 from pathlib import Path
 
-from agent_loom.pack.core import install_pack, uninstall_pack, update_pack
+from agent_loom.pack.core import (
+    doctor,
+    install_pack,
+    status,
+    uninstall_pack,
+    update_pack,
+)
 from agent_loom.pack.lock import lock_path
 from agent_loom.pack.packs import list_pack_ids, load_manifest
+from agent_loom.pack.lock import load_lock
 
 
 def test_pack_lists_builtin_sample_pack() -> None:
@@ -50,6 +57,65 @@ def test_pack_update_respects_drift_without_force(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
     assert ".opencode/commands/pack-sample.md" in res.skipped
 
 
+def test_pack_install_records_drifted_managed_files_in_lock(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
+    repo = tmp_path
+
+    # Pre-create a managed file with conflicting contents before install.
+    p = repo / ".opencode" / "commands" / "pack-sample.md"
+    p.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+    p.write_text("preexisting drift\n", encoding="utf-8")
+
+    res = install_pack(repo_root=repo, pack_id="sample", dry_run=False, force=False)
+    assert ".opencode/commands/pack-sample.md" in res.drifted
+
+    lock = load_lock(repo)
+    ip = next(p for p in lock.packs if p.id == "sample")
+    assert any(e.path == ".opencode/commands/pack-sample.md" for e in ip.files)
+
+    st = status(repo)
+    drifted_val = st.get("drifted")
+    assert (drifted_val if isinstance(drifted_val, int) else 0) == 1
+    doc = doctor(repo, pack_id="sample")
+    assert doc.get("ok") is False
+    res0 = doc.get("results")
+    assert isinstance(res0, list) and res0
+    r0 = res0[0]
+    assert isinstance(r0, dict)
+    drifted = r0.get("drifted")
+    assert isinstance(drifted, list)
+    assert ".opencode/commands/pack-sample.md" in drifted
+
+
+def test_pack_update_records_untracked_drifted_managed_files_in_lock(
+    tmp_path: Path,
+) -> None:
+    repo = tmp_path
+    install_pack(repo_root=repo, pack_id="sample", dry_run=False)
+
+    # Create drift and then simulate a legacy lock missing the entry.
+    rel = ".opencode/commands/pack-sample.md"
+    p = repo / rel
+    p.write_text("drift\n", encoding="utf-8")
+
+    lp = lock_path(repo)
+    doc = json.loads(lp.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
+    packs = doc.get("packs")
+    assert isinstance(packs, list)
+    for pr in packs:
+        if isinstance(pr, dict) and pr.get("id") == "sample":
+            files = pr.get("files")
+            if isinstance(files, list):
+                pr["files"] = [f for f in files if f.get("path") != rel]
+    lp.write_text(json.dumps(doc, indent=2, sort_keys=True) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
+
+    res = update_pack(repo_root=repo, pack_id="sample", dry_run=False, force=False)
+    assert rel in res.drifted
+
+    lock = load_lock(repo)
+    ip = next(p for p in lock.packs if p.id == "sample")
+    assert any(e.path == rel for e in ip.files)
+
+
 def test_pack_update_overwrites_drift_with_force(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
     repo = tmp_path
     install_pack(repo_root=repo, pack_id="sample", dry_run=False)

instinct_candidates.action: Add/adjust lifecycle tests to lock in invariants (happy path + edge cases) in `tests/test_pack_lifecycle.py`; make tests assert durable behaviors (state transitions, idempotency, cleanup, and failure modes) rather than incidental implementation details; run `uv run ruff check .`, `uv run basedpyright`, then `uv run pytest`.
instinct_candidates.title: Pack Changes Need Lifecycle Coverage
instinct_candidates.trigger: When you modify pack core behavior (selection, creation, install/activate, lifecycle/cleanup) or touch `src/agent_loom/pack/core.py`

instinct_candidates.action: Add/update focused CLI UX tests in `tests/test_pack_cli_ux.py` that assert exit code and a few key stdout/stderr substrings; avoid brittle full-output snapshots; keep assertions resilient to formatting/ordering changes while still protecting the user contract.
instinct_candidates.title: Lock Pack CLI UX With Low-Noise Tests
instinct_candidates.trigger: When you change pack-related CLI commands (flags, help text, user-visible messaging, exit codes) or add new pack UX behavior

instinct_candidates.action: Update core logic and tests together: core/lifecycle invariants in `tests/test_pack_lifecycle.py`, user-facing contract in `tests/test_pack_cli_ux.py`; ensure failures are informative (actionable error messages) and consistent with expected exit codes.
instinct_candidates.title: Treat Pack UX + Core As One Contract
instinct_candidates.trigger: When making a pack behavior change that has both a core and CLI surface area
